gaps.title: Context Window Optimization / Tool Schema Compression
gaps.description: Zero tools exist to address context window bloat from MCP tool schemas. Teams report burning 72% of context (143K of 200K tokens) on tool definitions alone. No tool compresses, deduplicates, or dynamically loads tool schemas based on task context.
gaps.why_gap_exists: The problem only surfaces at scale with multiple MCP servers connected. Early adopters used 1-3 servers. Now teams connect 10-20+, making context exhaustion a production blocker.

gaps.title: MCP Server CI/CD Testing Pipeline
gaps.description: No integrated CI/CD pipeline exists for MCP servers. MCP Inspector is dev-only (no CI mode). MCPevals provides GitHub Actions but only for evaluation, not full integration/regression testing. No tool offers automated protocol compliance testing, backward compatibility checks, or deployment gates.
gaps.why_gap_exists: MCP ecosystem is young; most servers are still in early/hobby stage. As production deployments grow, CI/CD becomes essential.
evidence_sources: Developer blog posts on testing gaps

gaps.title: Context-Aware Dynamic Tool Discovery
gaps.description: No tool enables agents to dynamically discover and load relevant MCP tools based on current task context. Current discovery is static: connect servers, get all tools. Need: 'I'm working on a Jira ticket' automatically surfaces relevant tools while hiding irrelevant ones.
gaps.why_gap_exists: MCP Server Cards are on the 2026 roadmap but not yet implemented. No third-party has built context-aware routing.

gaps.title: MCP-Native Distributed Tracing
gaps.description: No standardized way to trace requests across multiple MCP servers in a chain. Developers report troubleshooting that takes minutes with gRPC stretches to days with MCP. No correlation IDs, no request tracking, no multi-hop visibility.
gaps.why_gap_exists: MCP spec has no distributed tracing primitive. Existing APM tools don't understand MCP protocol semantics.

gaps.title: Push Notifications / Event Streaming for MCP
gaps.description: MCP operates purely on request-response. No push capability exists for real-time events (new emails, build completions, PR comments). Every integration requiring push must implement custom polling or webhooks outside the protocol.
gaps.why_gap_exists: Not in the MCP spec. The protocol was designed for tool calling, not event streaming. Requires protocol-level change or middleware layer.

gaps.title: MCP Server Quality Assurance & Vetting Platform
gaps.description: Reddit reports '95% of MCP servers are garbage.' No comprehensive quality vetting platform exists. Glama has scorecards but limited to security/licensing. Need: functional testing, reliability scoring, performance benchmarks, community ratings with verified usage.
gaps.why_gap_exists: Similar to early npm: low barrier to publish, no quality gates. Registry prioritizes quantity over quality.

gaps.title: Multi-Tenant MCP Server Framework
gaps.description: No framework or toolkit for building multi-tenant MCP servers. Every enterprise team building shared MCP infrastructure must implement tenant isolation, per-tenant auth, usage tracking, and rate limiting from scratch.
gaps.why_gap_exists: MCP was designed for single-user local workflows. Enterprise multi-tenancy is an emergent requirement as organizations centralize MCP infrastructure.

gaps.title: MCP Server Persistent Memory / State Management
gaps.description: No tool provides persistent memory across MCP sessions. Agents lose all context between sessions. No standard for storing conversation history, user preferences, or accumulated knowledge across MCP interactions.
gaps.why_gap_exists: MCP is stateless by design. Memory/persistence is considered an application concern, but no tooling exists to make it easy.

gaps.title: Unified MCP Server Management Dashboard
gaps.description: No single tool provides a unified view of all connected MCP servers with health status, usage metrics, cost tracking, and configuration management. Enterprises managing 50+ MCP connections have no centralized control plane.
gaps.why_gap_exists: Each MCP server is independent. No aggregation layer or management protocol exists.

gaps.title: MCP Server Supply Chain Security Scanner
gaps.description: 8,000+ MCP servers exposed with admin panels and debug endpoints. No dedicated supply chain security scanner for MCP servers that checks for prompt injection vulnerabilities, exposed credentials, malicious tool descriptions, and dependency risks.
gaps.why_gap_exists: MCP-specific attack vectors (prompt injection via tool descriptions, tool poisoning) are novel. Traditional security scanners don't understand MCP semantics.

gaps.title: Cross-Language MCP Server Scaffolding & Templates
gaps.description: No unified scaffolding tool generates production-ready MCP servers across multiple languages (TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust, Java) with consistent structure, testing, CI/CD, and deployment configs. Each SDK has its own patterns.
gaps.why_gap_exists: Each language SDK evolved independently. No cross-language consistency effort exists.
evidence_sources: Developer blog posts on building MCP servers in different languages

gaps.title: MCP Server Cost & Token Usage Optimization
gaps.description: No tool tracks and optimizes the cost of MCP tool calls including token consumption per tool, redundant calls, and cost attribution. With context window exhaustion burning tokens on schemas, cost visibility is critical.
gaps.why_gap_exists: Cost is an emergent concern as MCP moves from dev/hobby to production at scale. No tooling has been purpose-built for this.
